_meta.purpose: Hidden knowledge for NPC only — NOT given to main LLM

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, I am registered as self-employed for AHV purposes. I registered as a sole proprietor (Einzelunternehmen) when I started freelancing seriously — my first year doing it properly. My freelance work is ML consulting on weekends.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Confirms income.selfemployment.enabled = true. The LLM often misses setting this boolean flag.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, Swiss Re has a subsidized canteen on site. I eat there most days — it's very convenient.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Confirms Lohnausweis field 14.1 = Ja → verpflegung deduction CHF 1,600 applies

verbal_knowledge.answer: For my US dividends from Interactive Brokers, I used the ESTV official year-end exchange rate. The rate I used was approximately 0.88 CHF per USD — I looked it up on the ESTV website for December 31, 2025.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: USD 1,200 gross dividends × 0.88 = CHF 1,056. This is what should be declared as dividend income.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I pay AHV contributions separately as a self-employed person on my freelance income. The Ausgleichskasse bills me directly for that.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Freelance AHV contributions are deductible as part of self-employment expenses or separately.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I had four freelance ML consulting clients this year. The invoices total CHF 22,000 for the year — you can see them in my invoice documents.

My main freelance expenses are: the MacBook Pro I bought in March for CHF 1,800 (I have the receipt), software subscriptions like GitHub Copilot and AWS totaling CHF 600 (receipts available), and some small home office items like a monitor stand and cables for about CHF 300 — I didn't keep receipts for those small items though.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Total expenses CHF 2,700. Small items without receipts (CHF 300) may still be accepted. Net income = CHF 19,300.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I did sell some stocks this year through Interactive Brokers — made about USD 3,200 in gains. But my financial advisor told me that capital gains from private investing are tax-free in Switzerland, so I don't need to declare that as income, right?
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Correct — private capital gains are not taxable in Switzerland. Only dividends are declared as income. Do NOT add USD 3,200 as income.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I commute by public transport — I have an SBB Halbtax card but not a Jahresabo. I buy individual tickets or use the app. Actually, I don't think I have an annual pass — I should check if I can still claim commuting costs.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: No annual pass → no Fahrkosten deduction. Individual tickets are not deductible. Halbtax is a discount card, not a commuting receipt.

verbal_knowledge.answer: I rent my apartment on Militärstrasse. I don't own any property in Switzerland.
I donated CHF 500 to Médecins Sans Frontières this year — I support their work in conflict zones.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: CHF 500 donation to MSF qualifies (above CHF 100 threshold).

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, I took a German B2 course at Migros Klubschule this year. It cost CHF 890. I need good German for my client meetings at Swiss Re and for the Swiss tax forms!
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: CHF 890 Weiterbildungskosten for work-related language course — deductible.

verbal_knowledge.answer: Yes, I contribute to my Pillar 3a at Finpension. I put in the maximum I'm allowed — CHF 7,056 this year.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Finpension confirmation = CHF 7,056 (maximum for BVG-insured employees)

verbal_knowledge.answer: My Interactive Brokers portfolio is worth about USD 45,000 at the end of the year. I have US stocks and ETFs — Apple, Microsoft, VTI, Nvidia, Tesla, Amazon.
verbal_knowledge.tax_relevance: Securities portfolio USD 45,000 converted to CHF at year-end rate (~0.88) = ~CHF 39,600 for wealth declaration.

clarifications.answer: Yes, Swiss Re has a subsidized canteen and I use it most days. On my Lohnausweis, field 14.1 says Ja. So I should get the CHF 1,600 verpflegung deduction.
I'm not sure how to calculate the Berufsauslagen Pauschale exactly — you need to take 3% of my net salary from the Lohnausweis.
Yes, I definitely have self-employment income from my ML consulting. I registered for it. You need to mark that I am self-employed as a side activity.
clarifications.tax_relevance: income.selfemployment.enabled must be set to true (boolean). This is often missed.

clarifications.answer: My Interactive Brokers dividends were USD 1,200 gross for the year. At 0.88 CHF/USD that's about CHF 1,056 in Swiss francs.
clarifications.tax_relevance: CHF 1,056 in dividend income to declare. Capital gains (USD 3,200) are NOT taxable.
